steps.title: Spot the singular possession requirement
steps.content: The blank precedes “compelling narrative” and needs to show that this narrative belongs to the singular noun “the epic poem Raghuvaṃśa”.

steps.title: Choose the singular possessive pronoun
steps.content:
Its is the correct singular possessive pronoun for the singular subject.

The other three fail: their is plural (but the poem is singular), while they're (meaning “they are”) and it's (meaning “it is”) are contractions that introduce verbs where we only need possession.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
